criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output only explicitly states two of the five required dates: the examination commenced March 11, 2024 (mentioned in Allegation 6) and the referral notice dated November 6, 2024 (in the header). The deletion instruction date of April 22, 2024 is mentioned but is not one of the five required dates. The deficiency letter date (August 2, 2024), the referral to Enforcement date (October 18, 2024), and the service date (November 12, 2024) are not mentioned anywhere in the output. Since fewer than three of the five required dates are correctly stated, this fails the criterion.

criteria_results.title: Email Exhibit Language Quoted or Paraphrased
criteria_results.reasoning: The criterion requires that the output reference the Whitmore-to-Delgado email and note its ambiguous language as 'potentially supporting an innocent interpretation.' While the agent's output does reference the email and quotes its language ('clean up the shared drive' and 'clean up of legacy files on the shared drive'), it does not present this language as potentially ambiguous or as supporting an innocent interpretation. Instead, the output treats the email purely as incriminating evidence of obstructive intent, stating it is 'direct documentary evidence' and that 'the timing of the instruction...supports a strong inference of obstructive intent.' The criterion specifically requires noting the ambiguous nature of the language as potentially supporting an innocent interpretation, which the output fails to do.
